ops.title: CI guardrails: two swift-job incidents behind ci.yml's concurrency and timeouts
ops.body:
# Incident 1 — cancel-in-progress on main killed swift verdicts

ci.yml's concurrency block cancels superseded runs on PRs ONLY. On main, an in-progress run always finishes (new pushes queue; GitHub keeps just the newest pending run). When cancel-in-progress applied to main, rapid pushes repeatedly killed the slow macos swift job mid-run, leaving commits with no complete CI verdict. Do not "simplify" the concurrency block to cancel everywhere.

# Incident 2 — a hung swift test burned 4.9 idle hours

Every job carries timeout-minutes: each is sub-minute when caches hit, and the timeouts exist so a wedged test dies in minutes with logs instead of running to GitHub's 6-hour kill. A hung `swift test` once burned 4.9 idle hours. Do not drop the timeouts as redundant.

(Context also in ci.yml one-line comments; the codegen drift gate re-runs wire/lifeproto/gen and fails on any diff, proving the two generated bindings stay in sync with spec.go.)
